pillars.name: Hexagonal / Ports-Adapters Architecture
pillars.rationale: MessagingAdapter trait exists (crates/sidekick-messaging/src/lib.rs:64-70) but zero adapter implementations exist in tree. No infra layer, no adapter registry.
pillars.remediation: Implement at least one real adapter (IMessageAdapter or SmsAdapter) behind the trait. Add adapter registry with dynamic dispatch.

pillars.name: Single Responsibility / Cohesion
pillars.rationale: sidekick-messaging crate is small (96 LOC) and focused. However, README claims 4 canonical members (sidekick-messaging, sidekick-dispatch, sidekick-presence, cheap-llm-mcp) but only 1 is a workspace member.
pillars.remediation: Reconcile README member list with Cargo.toml workspace members. Either add missing crates or demote README claims.

pillars.name: Open/Closed Principle
pillars.rationale: MessagingAdapter trait provides an open extension point for new providers. Zero production adapters registered.
pillars.remediation: Implement at least one adapter; consider adapter registry for dynamic provider discovery.

pillars.name: Dependency Inversion
pillars.rationale: Trait abstraction exists in principle, but Cargo.toml has an unused path dependency on phenotype-errors (Cargo.toml:22) that couples the workspace to a cross-repo path.
pillars.remediation: Remove unused workspace dependencies. Publish shared crates to crates.io or use git dependencies instead of path deps.

pillars.name: DRY — Docs vs Code Alignment
pillars.rationale: README.md:37-47 claims 4 crate members; Cargo.toml:2-4 declares only sidekick-messaging. docs/consolidation_notes.md:11-14 lists crates absent from tree. No single SSOT for membership.
pillars.remediation: Establish single SSOT for workspace membership (Cargo.toml). Update all docs to match. Delete consolidation_notes.md or rewrite from Cargo.toml.

pillars.name: Module Boundaries / Workspace Hygiene
pillars.rationale: Only sidekick-messaging is a workspace member. sidekick-obs-core, sidekick-observability, and sidekick-dispatch exist under crates/ but are excluded. vendored cheap-llm-mcp is gitignored.
pillars.remediation: Add all crates under crates/ to workspace members, or move them out of crates/ to avoid ambiguity.

pillars.name: Coupling / Cohesion Balance
pillars.rationale: Messaging crate self-contained with minimal deps (serde, thiserror). However, workspace declares path dep to ../pheno/crates/phenotype-errors which is unused — creates unnecessary cross-repo coupling.
pillars.remediation: Remove unused deps. Consider publishing sidekick-messaging independently of workspace.

pillars.name: Dependency Direction (Inward)
pillars.rationale: Workspace Cargo.toml:20-22 declares anyhow and phenotype-errors as workspace deps. Neither is consumed by sidekick-messaging. Two dead deps increase cognitive load.
pillars.remediation: Remove anyhow and phenotype-errors from workspace.dependencies. Only declare deps that are actually used.

pillars.name: Layered Architecture (Domain/App/Infra)
pillars.rationale: Flat lib.rs pattern — no domain/, ports/, or adapters/ module separation. All logic lives in a single module file.
pillars.remediation: Restructure sidekick-messaging into domain/, ports/, and adapters/ modules. Separate domain types from I/O concerns.

pillars.name: No God Objects / Monoliths
pillars.rationale: Main lib.rs is 96 lines with clear types. No god objects in the Rust side. Python server.py has module-level globals (_router, _ledger) which add risk.
pillars.remediation: Inject Python globals as dependencies rather than module-level singletons.

pillars.name: Cyclic Dependency Freedom
pillars.rationale: No cycles in Rust dependency graph. Workspace has simple star topology — each crate standalone with no inter-crate deps.
pillars.remediation: Maintain current no-cycle policy. Use cargo-deny or CI check to prevent regressions.

pillars.name: Public Surface Minimalism
pillars.rationale: Small, focused public API for sidekick-messaging (Message, MessageProvider, MessagingAdapter trait, MessagingError). release-registry.toml lists crates not in tree.
pillars.remediation: Sync release-registry.toml with actual in-tree crates. Keep public surface small.

pillars.name: Dead Flexibility / Unused Abstraction
pillars.rationale: release-registry.toml:10-24 lists dispatch and presence crates as 'stub' and 'alpha' but they are absent from crates/. MessagingAdapter trait has no implementations beyond test stubs.
pillars.remediation: Either import the referenced crates or remove them from registry. Add adapter implementations or mark trait as experimental.

pillars.name: YAGNI Discipline
pillars.rationale: MessagingAdapter trait before a second implementation exists — YAGNI risk. However, the workspace is young and trait-first is defensible for a message abstraction.
pillars.remediation: Document trait design intent. If only 1 provider for v1, consider inlining the trait until 2nd provider emerges.

pillars.name: Invariants Encoded In Types
pillars.rationale: Message struct (lib.rs:41-46) allows empty body and empty recipient. No non-empty validation at construction.
pillars.remediation: Add NonEmptyString newtype with TryFrom<&str> validation. Use it in Message fields.

pillars.name: Illegal States Unrepresentable
pillars.rationale: MessageProvider enum prevents invalid combo strings but provider/recipient pairing isn't validated (e.g. Email provider with a phone number allowed).
pillars.remediation: Add newtype Recipient types per provider channel (RecipientEmail, E164Phone). Match provider variant to recipient type at construction.

pillars.name: Newtypes Over Primitives
pillars.rationale: recipient: String, body: String (lib.rs:43-44). No domain newtypes for any field.
pillars.remediation: Introduce Recipient, MessageBody, MessageId newtypes. Each newtype validates at construction.

pillars.name: Ubiquitous Language
pillars.rationale: MessageProvider::IMessage, SMS, Email clearly named. README uses 'sidekick-presence' and 'sidekick-dispatch' names not present in Cargo.toml members.
pillars.remediation: Align glossary across README, docs, and Cargo.toml. Drop references to crates not in workspace.

pillars.name: Enum Exhaustiveness
pillars.rationale: Display match on MessageProvider covers all 3 variants (lib.rs:31-37). No wildcard arms. serde uses exhaustive derive.
pillars.remediation: Consider adding #[non_exhaustive] if providers will be added by downstream crates.

pillars.name: Error Type Design
pillars.rationale: MessagingError enum with thiserror derives (lib.rs:14-20). Clear error messages. ProviderUnavailable and Unauthorized variants.
pillars.remediation: Wire Unauthorized into actual adapter implementations. Consider adding a third variant for transport errors.

pillars.name: Option/Result Discipline
pillars.rationale: Result<T> alias defined (lib.rs:11). No ? propagation paths currently because adapters are stubs.
pillars.remediation: Implement real adapters with ? propagation. Add From impls for upstream error types.

pillars.name: No Stringly-Typed IDs
pillars.rationale: Plain String for recipient and body. No MessageId type. No way to reference messages externally.
pillars.remediation: Add MessageId(Uuid) newtype. Return MessageId from MessagingAdapter::send instead of String.

pillars.name: Value vs Entity Distinction
pillars.rationale: Message is a value object (no identity). No Conversation or Thread aggregate model, which would be needed for multi-turn messaging.
pillars.remediation: Model Thread aggregate if conversational use case emerges. Document as value object for now.

pillars.name: ID Schemes / External Correlation
pillars.rationale: No message ID scheme. No way to correlate sent messages to provider-side receipts. correlation module exists in obs-core but not wired.
pillars.remediation: Define MessageId as UUID v4. Document external provider ID mapping strategy (e.g. provider_message_id field).

pillars.name: Serde Contracts
pillars.rationale: Serialize + Deserialize derived on domain types (lib.rs:42). No schema versioning. No deny_unknown_fields.
pillars.remediation: Add #[serde(deny_unknown_fields)] on structs receiving external input. Add version field to persisted schemas.

pillars.name: Clone/Debug Conventions
pillars.rationale: All public types derive Debug + Clone + PartialEq + Eq where appropriate, per CLAUDE.md convention.
pillars.remediation: Maintain on all new types. Consider adding Serialize bounds where serialization is expected.

pillars.name: Python Typed Models
pillars.rationale: Python config.py uses @dataclass for config models. But the broader cheap-llm-mcp codebase mixes typed and untyped patterns.
pillars.remediation: Extend dataclass usage to runtime request/response models. Consider pydantic for validation.

pillars.name: Validation at Construction
pillars.rationale: Message::new (lib.rs:50-60) accepts any Into<String> with no trim or non-empty checks. Empty body or recipient accepted silently.
pillars.remediation: Add try_new() -> Result<Self> with validation. Move validation upstream of construction.

pillars.name: REST Resource Modeling
pillars.rationale: No HTTP API in workspace. Entire surface is MCP-tool based. No route design, no HTTP verbs.
pillars.remediation: Document that Sidekick is MCP-native (not REST). If HTTP API is needed, design resource model around messaging/dispatch.

pillars.name: CLI Ergonomics
pillars.rationale: cheap-llm-mcp CLI includes doctor subcommand and help. Rust side provides sidekick-healthcheck binary. No unified CLI wrapper.
pillars.remediation: Add root CLI (sidekick CLI) with subcommands for health, messaging, dispatch. Document in README quickstart.

pillars.name: Versioning Policy
pillars.rationale: Workspace version 0.0.1 (Cargo.toml:8). Python pyproject.toml uses 0.4.0. Version skew between Rust and Python packages.
pillars.remediation: Adopt unified version policy for the collection. Consider independent semver per crate with workspace tracking.

pillars.name: Request/Response Contracts
pillars.rationale: MessagingAdapter::send returns bare String (lib.rs:66) instead of structured SendReceipt. MCP tools return typed dicts on Python side.
pillars.remediation: Define SendReceipt struct with message_id, provider_status, timestamp. Use in trait return type.

pillars.name: Idempotency Support
pillars.rationale: No idempotency keys. Retry may duplicate sends when MessagingAdapter is wired to real providers.
pillars.remediation: Add optional idempotency_key: Option<String> to Message. Document deduplication strategy if applicable.

pillars.name: Pagination Strategy
pillars.rationale: No list/query APIs that require pagination. Acceptable for current scope but needs documentation.
pillars.remediation: Document pagination as N/A in API spec. If list endpoints added, use cursor-based pagination.

pillars.name: HTTP Status Semantics
pillars.rationale: Python retry.py:15 defines RETRYABLE_STATUS set. Code distinguishes retryable vs terminal errors. Rust side has no HTTP layer.
pillars.remediation: Expose retry policy in docs. Map MessagingError variants to HTTP status codes where applicable.

pillars.name: Backward Compatibility
pillars.rationale: cargo-semver-checks.yml configured for sidekick-messaging only. Python MCP tools not covered. Pre-1.0, breaking changes likely.
pillars.remediation: Add semver-check for all workspace crates. Document compatibility policy for Python MCP surface.

pillars.name: Schema Documentation (OpenAPI/MCP)
pillars.rationale: No OpenAPI spec. MCP tool schemas are in code only — no generated manifest published. no docs/reference for MCP surface.
pillars.remediation: Generate MCP tool schema manifest (JSON Schema). Publish in docs/ during CI build.

pillars.name: Input Contracts & Validation
pillars.rationale: MCP tools use typed parameters with Literal provider params (server.py:52). Config TOML parsed with validation. Rust trait accepts any Message.
pillars.remediation: Add input validation at trait boundary in Rust. Derive validation specs from MCP tool schemas.

pillars.name: MCP Tool Surface Completeness
pillars.rationale: complete, health, stream_complete tools exist in Python MCP server. Messaging crate has no MCP exposure — no bridge layer.
pillars.remediation: Bridge sidekick-messaging to MCP tools or document deferral in roadmap.

pillars.name: Trait API Stability
pillars.rationale: Pre-1.0 stubs (release-registry.toml:21 status=stub). Breaking changes expected. No versioning guarantees published.
pillars.remediation: Stabilize MessagingAdapter trait before 0.1.0. Document experimental vs stable portions of API.

pillars.name: Error Mapping to API Surface
pillars.rationale: MessagingError not exposed via MCP. Consumers cannot distinguish error types programmatically. Python side has partial error codes.
pillars.remediation: Define error codes for each MessagingError variant. Include in MCP tool error responses.

pillars.name: Cost/Rate API Exposure
pillars.rationale: Ledger.check_cap() in Python server tracks monthly spend. Rust side has no cost tracking. No user-facing quota API.
pillars.remediation: Surface cost/rate information through health endpoint. Document ledger caps in README.

pillars.name: Unit Test Coverage
pillars.rationale: 34 #[test] functions total across 14 test files (including stubs). Messaging lib has 3 inline tests. obs-core has solid module-level tests.
pillars.remediation: Expand unit tests to cover all public functions. Target 80%+ coverage on core logic.

pillars.name: Integration Tests
pillars.rationale: obs-core integration.rs tests cross-module interactions. Messaging stubs are contract-level only. No real adapter integration tests.
pillars.remediation: Add integration tests exercising MessagingAdapter through real trait dispatch. Use mock providers.

pillars.name: End-to-End Tests
pillars.rationale: No cross-crate E2E harness. No MCP round-trip test in CI. Smoke test (tests/smoke_test.rs) is trivial 2+2=4 assertion.
pillars.remediation: Build E2E: clone → build → run healthcheck → MCP tool round-trip. Gate on CI.

pillars.name: Property-Based Tests
pillars.rationale: No proptest or quickcheck dependency. Zero property-based tests in the codebase.
pillars.remediation: Add proptest for Message serialization round-trip, MessagingAdapter contract invariants.

pillars.name: BDD / Acceptance Tests
pillars.rationale: No Gherkin, no BDD framework. No .feature files.
pillars.remediation: Optional — only if stakeholder acceptance requires behavior-driven scenarios.

pillars.name: Coverage Tracking & Enforcement
pillars.rationale: No coverage tool configured. docs/reference/fr_coverage_matrix.md reports zero FRs. No CI gate on coverage.
pillars.remediation: Add cargo-tarpaulin or llvm-cov. Publish coverage report in CI. Set minimum coverage threshold.

pillars.name: Meaningful Assertions
pillars.rationale: Smoke test (tests/smoke_test.rs:7) asserts 2+2=4. Several stubs have basic true/false checks. Some deeper assertions in obs-core tests.
pillars.remediation: Replace trivial smoke test with workspace invariant. Strengthen stub assertions to validate contract semantics.

pillars.name: Fixtures / Factories / Test Helpers
pillars.rationale: Python has conftest.py with fixtures. Rust side has no shared test utilities, no MessageBuilder pattern.
pillars.remediation: Add shared test module with MessageBuilder, StubAdapter factory, and common test assertions.

pillars.name: Determinism & Isolation
pillars.rationale: No random values used in Rust tests. Stubs have no external dependencies. Python tests use httpx mocks.
pillars.remediation: Seed RNG in tests if randomization is added. Maintain no-network-required test patterns.

pillars.name: Test Isolation / No Shared State
pillars.rationale: Rust tests are well-isolated with independent stubs. Python server.py globals present risk of cross-test pollution.
pillars.remediation: Reset module-level globals between Python tests. Use dependency injection instead of globals.

pillars.name: Mutation Resistance
pillars.rationale: No mutation testing configured. With minimal test coverage, mutation survival would be high.
pillars.remediation: Add cargo-mutants or similar once coverage baseline is established.

pillars.name: Performance / Load Tests
pillars.rationale: No benchmark harness. No criterion benches. No Python benchmarking.
pillars.remediation: Add criterion benchmarks for send path and provider resolution. Add pytest-benchmark for MCP tools.

pillars.name: Contract Tests
pillars.rationale: Stub adapters define contract. Python has @pytest.mark.requirement markers. Rust lacks FR→test linkage annotations.
pillars.remediation: Adopt #[fr("FR-MSG-001")] proc-macro or document FR→test mapping in test comments.

pillars.name: Flaky-Free CI Pipeline
pillars.rationale: Rust CI passes consistently. Python suite not gated in CI — risk of drift. No network-dependent tests currently.
pillars.remediation: Add Python job to ci.yml with uv run pytest. Keep all tests network-free with mocks.

pillars.name: Pipeline Completeness
pillars.rationale: 11 workflows: ci, cargo-deny, cargo-audit, cargo-machete, cargo-semver-checks, codeql, trufflehog, scorecard, docs, release-attestation, sbom-refresh. Strong breadth.
pillars.remediation: Add unified CI gate with all checks aggregated. Add Python workflow.

pillars.name: cargo fmt --check Gate
pillars.rationale: pre-commit-config.yaml:12 has fmt hook (client-side). ci.yml lacks cargo fmt --check. Formatting not enforced server-side.
pillars.remediation: Add cargo fmt --check step to ci.yml before clippy.

pillars.name: Clippy Gate
pillars.rationale: ci.yml:14 runs clippy with -D warnings. Consistent enforcement.
pillars.remediation: Keep. Consider adding clippy for Python (ruff) as well.

pillars.name: Build Matrix Coverage
pillars.rationale: All jobs run on ubuntu-latest only. No macOS or Windows testing. No Rust toolchain matrix (stable only).
pillars.remediation: Add OS matrix (macOS + Windows) for test job. Add nightly toolchain for forward-compat check.

pillars.name: Release Artifacts Pipeline
pillars.rationale: release-attestation.yml builds + stages artifacts. SLSA L2 attestation configured. Never triggered (0 tags, 0 releases).
pillars.remediation: Define first release tag. Verify release-attestation.yml produces correct artifacts for lib-only crate.

pillars.name: Scheduled / Nightly Workflows
pillars.rationale: Weekly cron on cargo-audit (Wed), cargo-deny (Mon), cargo-machete (Thu), scorecard (Sat), codeql (Tue). No nightly full suite.
pillars.remediation: Add nightly workflow running the full test matrix + all security scans.

pillars.name: E2E / Integration Workflow
pillars.rationale: No E2E workflow. No smoke test across Rust + Python boundary.
pillars.remediation: Add e2e.yml: build → start MCP server → run python MCP client → verify response.

pillars.name: Artifact Integrity & Signing
pillars.rationale: SLSA build attestation configured via slsa-framework for releases. Not yet exercised. Release pipeline assumes binary output but sidekick-messaging is lib-only.
pillars.remediation: Test attestation workflow. Add crate publishing step. Handle lib-only crate correctly.

pillars.name: Cache Strategy
pillars.rationale: Swatinem/rust-cache in main CI and release workflow. Cargo registry + target directory cached. No Python/uv cache.
pillars.remediation: Add uv/pip cache if Python CI is added.

pillars.name: Required Checks Documented
pillars.rationale: CLAUDE.md lists quality gates (fmt, clippy, test). README references them. Branch protection not in-repo.
pillars.remediation: Document required checks in CONTRIBUTING.md with explicit list. Add branch protection rules file.

pillars.name: Rollback Procedure
pillars.rationale: No rollback procedure documented. No revert strategy for crate publishes or deployments.
pillars.remediation: Write one-page rollback runbook covering: crate revert, git revert, tag removal.

pillars.name: Changelog / Release Notes
pillars.rationale: CHANGELOG.md present (37 lines) but Unreleased section is empty. No release notes for any version (0 tags).
pillars.remediation: Maintain changelog entries per PR. Generate release notes on tag push (cliff.toml or similar).

pillars.name: CI Cost Efficiency
pillars.rationale: All jobs on free-tier ubuntu-latest. Cargo caching reduces rebuild time. Workflows use minimal compute.
pillars.remediation: Keep free tier. Monitor GitHub Actions minutes as matrix expands.

pillars.name: Python / Polyglot CI
pillars.rationale: No pytest, ruff, mypy, or Python CI job. 52 Python files in tree are ungated. No uv.lock or pyproject.toml at root.
pillars.remediation: Add Python CI job with uv sync, ruff check, pytest. Gate on cheap-llm-mcp tests.

pillars.name: Authentication Layer
pillars.rationale: MCP server has no auth layer (server.py). Open local trust model appropriate for stdio transport but undocumented.
pillars.remediation: Document threat model for local MCP trust. Add optional token-based auth for SSE transport.

pillars.name: Authorization Enforcement
pillars.rationale: MessagingError::Unauthorized variant exists (lib.rs:18-19) but never constructed. No authorization logic.
pillars.remediation: Enforce authorization when multi-tenant or when sensitive operations are added.

pillars.name: Secrets via Environment / No Hardcoding
pillars.rationale: API keys read from environment variables (config.py:20-24). .env.example under cheap-llm-mcp/. No secrets in Rust code.
pillars.remediation: Add root .env.example aggregating all env vars. Document required env vars per crate.

pillars.name: Dependency CVE Auditing
pillars.rationale: cargo-audit.yml runs weekly (Wed cron) plus on pushes to Cargo.toml/Cargo.lock. Uses rustsec/audit-check with GITHUB_TOKEN.
pillars.remediation: Keep. Consider adding pip-audit for Python dependencies.

pillars.name: Supply Chain / Pinned Actions
pillars.rationale: cargo-deny.yml, cargo-audit.yml, scorecard.yml use SHA-pinned actions. ci.yml uses @v4 and @v2 floating tags. trufflehog.yml uses @main (unpinned). Mixed.
pillars.remediation: Pin all actions to SHA digests. Use dependabot for SHA updates. Fix trufflehog.yml to use release tag not @main.

pillars.name: SBOM Management
pillars.rationale: sbom.cdx.json present. sbom-refresh.yml monthly cron. docs/security/sbom.json lists 68 components. CycloneDX format. Python SBOM not generated.
pillars.remediation: Add Python dependency SBOM. Fix sbom.json metadata (currently says 'FocalPoint' instead of 'Sidekick').

pillars.name: Input Validation at Boundaries
pillars.rationale: Config TOML validated (config.py:58-65). MCP tool prompt length not bounded. Rust inputs validated only through serde deserialization.
pillars.remediation: Add max prompt size guard for MCP tools. Add length validation in Message constructors.

pillars.name: Injection / Output Safety
pillars.rationale: No SQL injection risk. Prompts passed to upstream LLM providers with sanitization controlled by provider. No output sanitization on messages.
pillars.remediation: Add log redaction for sensitive fields. Consider prompt injection guard at MCP boundary.

pillars.name: TLS / Transport Security
pillars.rationale: Default URLs use HTTPS (config.py:80-102). Rust has no HTTP client yet.
pillars.remediation: Enforce TLS in custom provider configs. Document TLS expectations.

pillars.name: Least Privilege (CI Permissions)
pillars.rationale: Most workflows use permissions: read-all. Release workflow uses id-token: write (justified for attestation). Scorecard uses security-events: write.
pillars.remediation: Review per-workflow permissions. Add explicit permissions blocks to workflows missing them.

pillars.name: Rate Limiting / Cost Caps
pillars.rationale: Ledger implements monthly cost cap (server.py:64). Per-request rate limiting absent. Token bucket not implemented.
pillars.remediation: Add per-request rate limiter with configurable TPM/TPD. Surface rate limit status in health endpoint.

pillars.name: Secret Scanning
pillars.rationale: trufflehog.yml runs on push to main + PRs. Uses --only-verified. Unpinned @main tag is a risk.
pillars.remediation: Pin trufflehog to a release tag. Add gitleaks as complementary scanning.

pillars.name: CODEOWNERS Coverage
pillars.rationale: CODEOWNERS present with @KooshaPari as single owner for all patterns. Covers workflows, Cargo.toml, deny.toml. No backup owner.
pillars.remediation: Add backup reviewer/owner to CODEOWNERS patterns. Consider team-based ownership.

pillars.name: SAST / CodeQL Coverage
pillars.rationale: codeql.yml configured for Rust analysis only. Runs weekly + on push/PR to main. Python files not analyzed by CodeQL.
pillars.remediation: Add languages: python to CodeQL analysis matrix. Add explicit python analysis job.

pillars.name: Structured Logging
pillars.rationale: sidekick-observability crate provides init_tracing() with JSON output. Python has JsonFormatter. Neither is wired into application code.
pillars.remediation: Wire init_tracing() into sidekick-messaging binary entry point. Add tracing-subscriber as dependency.

pillars.name: Log Level Configuration
pillars.rationale: Rust sidekick_log_levels module provides LogLevel enum and env-driven config. Respects RUST_LOG. Python has CHEAP_LLM_LOG_LEVEL.
pillars.remediation: Document all env vars for log configuration in a single place. Add default level policies per crate.

pillars.name: Metrics Export (Prometheus)
pillars.rationale: Metrics module defines Counter/Histogram traits, no-op impl, and InMemoryMetrics for test. Standard names defined (METRIC_DISPATCH_TOTAL, etc.). No production export.
pillars.remediation: Implement MetricsRegistry backed by prometheus crate. Expose /metrics endpoint. Wire into sidekick-messaging.

pillars.name: Tracing / Spans
pillars.rationale: tracing dep declared in workspace Cargo.toml:19. sidekick-obs-core provides CorrelationContext. Zero #[instrument] attributes in codebase. Zero tracing! macros.
pillars.remediation: Add #[instrument] to MessagingAdapter::send. Create spans for each operation with crate, op, provider fields.

pillars.name: Health / Readiness Probes
pillars.rationale: HealthReport and ReadinessReport types in obs-core. sidekick-healthcheck binary exists. ComponentCheck for structured probe results.
pillars.remediation: Wire healthcheck into CI smoke test. Add readiness check for MCP server availability.

pillars.name: Error Reporting / Sinks
pillars.rationale: Basic JSON logging captures errors. No Sentry, Datadog, or external error sink configured. No panic hook for crash collection.
pillars.remediation: Add optional Sentry/error sink behind feature flag. Set up panic hook that logs panic details.

pillars.name: Correlation ID Propagation
pillars.rationale: CorrelationContext type provides request_id + trace_id. CorrelationStore for thread-safe propagation. Python has request_scope. Neither wired into production flow.
pillars.remediation: Propagate correlation_id through MCP tool invocations and dispatch boundaries. Log in every span.

pillars.name: Dashboards
pillars.rationale: No dashboard definitions in repo. No Grafana JSON, no Datadog dashboard config.
pillars.remediation: Add sample Grafana dashboard JSON for: dispatch throughput, error rate, latency p50/p95/p99.

pillars.name: Alerting Rules
pillars.rationale: No alerting configuration. No SLO burns alerts, no cost cap alerts, no error rate alerts.
pillars.remediation: Define at least 3 alert rules: cost cap breach, sustained error rate >5%, health check failure.

pillars.name: Audit Trail / Ledger
pillars.rationale: cheap-llm-mcp has JSONL ledger for cost tracking. Dispatch storage stubs define JSONL schema. Messaging sends not logged.
pillars.remediation: Add audit logging for message sends (recipient_hash, provider, status, timestamp).

pillars.name: Tracing Integration (Rust)
pillars.rationale: sidekick-observability crate built but not wired into sidekick-messaging or any binary. tracing-subscriber deps present but init not called.
pillars.remediation: Add sidekick-observability as dep to sidekick-messaging. Call init_tracing() at binary startup.

pillars.name: SLO Definitions
pillars.rationale: No service level objectives documented. No latency targets, availability targets, or error budgets.
pillars.remediation: Define initial SLO: MCP tool p99 latency < 5s, availability > 99.5%, error rate < 1%.

pillars.name: OpenTelemetry Export
pillars.rationale: No OpenTelemetry SDK or exporter configured. No distributed tracing across service boundaries.
pillars.remediation: Add opentelemetry-otlp behind feature flag. Configure optional export to OTel collector.

pillars.name: CI Failure Observability
pillars.rationale: Standard GitHub Actions logs. No test report artifacts uploaded. No build insights dashboard.
pillars.remediation: Upload test results as CI artifacts. Add JUnit report generation for Rust tests.

pillars.name: Hot-Path Profiling
pillars.rationale: No profiling data collected. No flamegraph or perf recording in docs or scripts.
pillars.remediation: Add cargo flamegraph script and profiling guide. Profile adapter send path and provider resolution.

pillars.name: Async/Concurrency Correctness
pillars.rationale: MessagingAdapter trait is sync (lib.rs:64). Python MCP server has async def complete (server.py:49). Hybrid sync/async may block event loop.
pillars.remediation: Consider making MessagingAdapter::send async (trait-variant or async-trait). Document blocking expectations.

pillars.name: Caching Strategy
pillars.rationale: Python TTL cache for provider responses (router.py:19,56-69). Rust side has no caching. Provider availability results uncached.
pillars.remediation: Cache provider availability checks in Rust. Add TTL-based cache with configurable expiry.

pillars.name: N+1 Query / Call Prevention
pillars.rationale: Provider fallback iterates sequentially (router.py:72-80). Health checks to all providers done sequentially, not parallel.
pillars.remediation: Run provider health probes in parallel with tokio::join! or futures::join_all.

pillars.name: Resource Bounds
pillars.rationale: max_tokens default 4096 in Python server. No global concurrency limit. No semaphore on inflight requests.
pillars.remediation: Add Semaphore-based concurrency limit. Define max_concurrent_requests config per provider.

pillars.name: Streaming vs Buffering
pillars.rationale: stream_complete MCP tool + streaming tests exist (test_streaming.py). Rust messaging has no streaming — acceptable for message sends.
pillars.remediation: Keep streaming in Python MCP. Document streaming deferral for Rust messaging crate.

pillars.name: Backpressure Mechanism
pillars.rationale: No backpressure mechanism. Unbounded prompt size. No queue depth limits for dispatch.
pillars.remediation: Add bounded channel for dispatch queue. Define max prompt size with configurable limit.

pillars.name: Algorithmic Complexity Awareness
pillars.rationale: O(providers) loop for provider fallback — fine at small scale (3-4 providers). Documented as linear.
pillars.remediation: Document scaling limits in architecture docs. Add early-exit optimization for healthy provider.

pillars.name: Load Ceiling Documentation
pillars.rationale: No load ceiling documented. No max RPS / concurrent MCP calls specified.
pillars.remediation: Document: max concurrent requests, max prompt tokens, recommended rate limits.

pillars.name: Memory Management
pillars.rationale: Python module-level caches (_router, _ledger) potential growth (server.py:18-19). Rust has minimal allocation.
pillars.remediation: Bound all caches with max_size. Add cache eviction policy. Document memory footprint.

pillars.name: Release Profile Tuning
pillars.rationale: Profile.release sets opt-level=3, lto=true (Cargo.toml:24-26). No fat LTO or PGO, but standard tuning for perf.
pillars.remediation: Consider fat LTO for single-crate release. Profile-guided optimization for binary deliverables.

pillars.name: HTTP Connection Reuse
pillars.rationale: Python httpx clients handle connection pooling. No explicit pool tuning or keep-alive configuration.
pillars.remediation: Configure httpx pool limits explicitly. Document connection reuse strategy.

pillars.name: Cost Efficiency Caps
pillars.rationale: monthly_cost_cap_usd configurable (config.py:32). Ledger enforces monthly budget check before dispatch.
pillars.remediation: Add per-request cost budgeting. Surface cost projections before expensive operations.

pillars.name: Benchmark Harness
pillars.rationale: No benchmark harness. No criterion dependency. No pytest-benchmark. No regression detection for performance changes.
pillars.remediation: Add criterion benchmark crate for MessagingAdapter::send. Add pytest-benchmark for MCP tools.

pillars.name: Schema Design & Documentation
pillars.rationale: Message struct is the primary schema (lib.rs:41-46). JSONL ledger schema documented in DATA.md. No formal schema definition language used.
pillars.remediation: Define formal schemas in DATA.md for all persisted data. Add JSON Schema files alongside ledger code.

pillars.name: Versioned Schema Migrations
pillars.rationale: No migration framework. No version history for data schemas. JSONL format could diverge between versions.
pillars.remediation: Add version field to all persisted entries. Create migration/ directory with reversible scripts.

pillars.name: Referential Integrity
pillars.rationale: No relational database. JSONL files are independent. No cross-file referential integrity. Acceptable for stateless design.
pillars.remediation: Document intentional lack of referential integrity in ADR. Add integrity checks if SQLite is adopted.

pillars.name: Indexing Strategy
pillars.rationale: No database, no indexes. JSONL files read linearly. No strategy for efficient query.
pillars.remediation: N/A until datastore is added. Document indexing plan if SQLite is used for dispatch.

pillars.name: Backup / Restore Procedures
pillars.rationale: No backup procedure. No restore documentation. Ledger data could be lost on crash or filesystem failure.
pillars.remediation: Document backup: cp ~/.cheap-llm/ledger.jsonl to backup location. Add optional WAL-based SQLite for crash safety.

pillars.name: Transaction Support
pillars.rationale: No transactional writes. JSONL append is atomic per-line but multiple lines aren't transactional. No write-ahead log.
pillars.remediation: Use SQLite for transactional writes where atomicity required. JSONL is acceptable for append-only audit logs.

pillars.name: Data Validation on Persist
pillars.rationale: Ledger append writes serde-validated data. No schema validation on read (JSONL deserialization). Corrupted entries crash.
pillars.remediation: Add schema validation on ledger read with skip-and-warn on corrupt entries. Version field for format detection.

pillars.name: Consistency Model Definition
pillars.rationale: Append-only JSONL — no crash safety spec. No fsync guarantee. No ACID properties.
pillars.remediation: Document consistency guarantees per storage component. Add fsync option for ledger writes.

pillars.name: Persistence Layer Abstraction
pillars.rationale: File path hardcoded in server.py:35. LedgerStore trait not defined. Not injectable for testing.
pillars.remediation: Define LedgerStore trait with FileLedger and InMemoryLedger impls. Inject via constructor, not global.

pillars.name: Serde / Serialization for Persistence
pillars.rationale: JSON lines format for ledger. Serde derived on Message. No version field per entry. No backward-compat fields.
pillars.remediation: Add version field to all persisted entries. Use #[serde(default)] for forward-compat fields.

pillars.name: Reversible Migrations
pillars.rationale: No migration system. No up/down scripts. Schema changes not reversible.
pillars.remediation: N/A until versioned store is needed. Add reversible migration scripts when SQLite is adopted.

pillars.name: Encryption at Rest
pillars.rationale: No encryption for persisted data. Ledger may contain metadata about prompts and usage. Recipient data in message fields.
pillars.remediation: Add optional encryption for sensitive ledger fields. Document when encryption is needed.

pillars.name: Retention Policy
pillars.rationale: Ledger grows unbounded (configurable monthly cap but no rotation/archival). No data lifecycle management.
pillars.remediation: Define retention: ledger compaction job, archival on rotation, deletion after N months.

pillars.name: ORM / SQL Abstraction
pillars.rationale: No ORM, no SQL. Intentionally stateless for messaging crate. Python uses JSONL. Acceptable for current design.
pillars.remediation: Document intentional omission of ORM. If SQLite added, consider sqlx for Rust.

pillars.name: README Quality & Completeness
pillars.rationale: README is 199 lines with work-state header, architecture section, usage examples, quality gates. Well-structured for a young project.
pillars.remediation: Add live CI badges. Ensure member table matches workspace. Add Python setup instructions.

pillars.name: Quickstart / Getting Started
pillars.rationale: docs/getting-started.md exists but clone-only flow fails due to path dependency on ../pheno. Python setup not integrated.
pillars.remediation: Make clone-and-build work standalone. Document or remove path deps. Add uv sync step for Python.

pillars.name: Install / Setup Documentation
pillars.rationale: rust-toolchain.toml pins toolchain. Taskfile.yml lists tasks. justfile imports shared recipes. Python setup via uv not documented at root.
pillars.remediation: Add root documentation section for Python dependencies. Document uv setup for cheap-llm-mcp.

pillars.name: API Reference Documentation
pillars.rationale: Rust doc comments on public items. No cargo doc published. MCP tools documented inline only. No rendered doc site for crate API.
pillars.remediation: Publish cargo doc via GitHub Pages. Add MCP tool reference doc in docs/.

pillars.name: Runnable Examples
pillars.rationale: No examples/ directory. README bash blocks untested in CI. No standalone example crate.
pillars.remediation: Create examples/ directory with runnable Rust + Python examples tested in CI.

pillars.name: Onboarding Experience
pillars.rationale: CONTRIBUTING.md is thin (23 lines). Devcontainer exists but CLAUDE.md quality gates not enforced in CI. Python env setup not documented.
pillars.remediation: Expand CONTRIBUTING.md with full setup guide: env vars, Python deps, Rust toolchain, CI gate overview.

pillars.name: CONTRIBUTING Quality
pillars.rationale: CONTRIBUTING.md present at 23 lines — covers basics but no code style section for Python, no PR checklist, no review process.
pillars.remediation: Add Python code style section (ruff conventions). Add PR checklist template. Link to code review policy.

pillars.name: Docs Site / VitePress
pillars.rationale: VitePress site with GitHub Pages deployment (docs.yml). docs/index.md, .vitepress/config.mjs configured. Several propagated stubs in intent/boundary.
pillars.remediation: Fill intent and boundary doc stubs (docs/intent/sidekick.md has <To be filled>). Add more sections to nav.

pillars.name: ADR Discipline
pillars.rationale: Zero ADRs in repository. docs/worklogs/ARCHITECTURE.md is a scaffold only. No architecturally-significant decisions documented.
pillars.remediation: Write ADR-001: workspace scope and stateless messaging design. ADR-002: MCP-only API surface.

pillars.name: FR/NFR Specification Quality
pillars.rationale: docs/FUNCTIONAL_REQUIREMENTS.md exists but uses SIDE-* IDs unrelated to actual code. cheap-llm-mcp has separate FR set. No unified FR registry.
pillars.remediation: Rewrite FR/NFR spec from actual domain. Consolidate cheap-llm-mcp and sidekick requirements into single registry.

pillars.name: Governance Artifacts Completeness
pillars.rationale: STATUS.md ✓, SSOT.md ✓, BRAND.md ✓, SUPPORT.md ✓, CITATION.cff ✓, CODE_OF_CONDUCT ✓, FUNDING.yml ✓, SECURITY.md ✓. Strong set of governance files.
pillars.remediation: Keep all artifacts current. Add quarterly review cadence for stale files.

pillars.name: Traceability (FR → Impl → Test)
pillars.rationale: Python tests use @pytest.mark.requirement markers. Rust tests lack FR annotations. Root FR spec IDs (SIDE-*) unrelated to cheap-llm-* FRs in Python.
pillars.remediation: Adopt FR ID convention used consistently across Rust + Python. Add CI check verifying FR↔test mapping.

pillars.name: No Orphan / Dead Code
pillars.rationale: 2 dead workspace deps: anyhow (Cargo.toml:20), phenotype-errors (Cargo.toml:22). release-registry.toml lists crates not in tree. docs reference absent crates.
pillars.remediation: Remove dead workspace deps. Sync release-registry.toml with actual tree. Fix docs referencing missing crates.

pillars.name: Audit Scorecard Accuracy
pillars.rationale: Existing audit_scorecard.json:39 says 'No source files found' — false negative. Many counters show 0 for existing data. Scorecard not regenerated from actual code.
pillars.remediation: Regenerate scorecard from live code analysis. Automate scorecard generation in CI or as Makefile target.

score_summary_note: Sidekick scores 41.6% (D+) on a 140-pillar / 10-domain taxonomy. Security is the strongest domain anchored by 11 CI workflows and solid security automation. Data & Persistence is weakest due to the intentionally stateless design without explicit ADR documentation. Testing and Observability also lag significantly — the observability crate exists but is not wired, and the 34 tests are mostly stubs. The project has strong governance artifacts (17 files) but suffers from doc-code drift (README claims crates not in workspace, dead deps).
